SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper states its main claim (learning classless association via an EM-like training that matches output distributions) and presents experiments on four MNIST-derived datasets that support those empirical claims (averages over 10 folds). The mathematical presentation of the method (Equations 1–5) is simple and plausible but lacks formal justification: the use of the elementwise power and the weighting vector γ is presented heuristically without convergence analysis or derivation showing this indeed yields a correct EM interpretation. The experimental methodology has weaknesses for strict technical validation: very large mini-batches (M=5250) and delayed pseudo-class updates are used without ablation, baselines are limited (only k-means, agglomerative clustering, and a supervised MLP), and no statistical tests or error bars are reported, so it is hard to judge robustness and significance of the reported gains.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper frames a reasonable variant of a known problem (association / multimodal grounding) and combines ideas seen in prior work (ODM / output-distribution matching, Siamese-style mutual supervision, and clustering/pseudo-label EM). The main novelty is the particular EM-like mechanism with a learnable weighting vector γ and the power-based pseudo-class extraction, but this is an incremental engineering variation rather than a clearly new theoretical framework. The relation to closely related methods (e.g., Sutskever et al.'s ODM and other pseudo-labeling or co-training approaches) is mentioned but not deeply contrasted, so the contribution is modestly novel but not surprising to an expert.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is organized in standard sections (intro, method, experiments, conclusion) and the main contributions are stated early. The notation around Equations (1)–(5) is mostly consistent and figures (e.g. Figure 4/5) are informative. However, there is noticeable repetition in the text, some minor typographical/formatting issues, and several methodological details (exact schedule for pseudo-class updates, rationale for huge mini-batch choice, full description of hyperparameter search) are under-specified, which makes reproducing the method harder than it should be.
